description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just pulled a month-worth of cloud-usage logs and now need to isolate the cost-increasing EC2 and RDS events for a quick optimisation review.

Initial data location
• A raw log file already exists at
  /home/user/finops/raw/usage-2023-09.log

Log format (comma-separated, single-line records)
TIMESTAMP,SERVICE,ACTION,COST_IMPACT,AMOUNT
Example:
2023-09-01T00:05:14Z,ec2,StartInstances,increase,4.27

Goal
1. Filter the raw log with a single extended-regex that keeps only the lines where
   a) the SERVICE field is ec2 or rds  (match is case-sensitive)
   AND
   b) the COST_IMPACT field is increase  (exact literal string)
2. Save those matched lines, in their original chronological order, to
   /home/user/finops/filtered/usage-2023-09-increase.log
3. Create a one-line summary file
   /home/user/finops/summary/increase-count.txt
   whose sole content is the decimal count of lines you just kept
   (no extra spaces, just the number and a trailing newline).

Output verification rules (read these carefully)
• /home/user/finops/filtered/usage-2023-09-increase.log must contain ONLY the wanted lines and must preserve the order found in the source file.
• /home/user/finops/summary/increase-count.txt must contain exactly one line with the count, e.g.
4⏎
• Do not add any header lines or extra blank lines.
• Achieve everything with at most three shell commands in total (directory creation commands do not count toward this limit).

Hints (do NOT reveal commands directly)
• You will need to create the filtered and summary directories first.
• A single egrep/grep -E expression can match both the service and the cost impact.
• Use a counting utility to produce the summary.

When you are finished, the two new files should be in place and formatted exactly as specified above.
truth:
Pre-existing directory tree and file:

Directory:
/home/user/finops/raw

File (10 lines total):
/home/user/finops/raw/usage-2023-09.log
Exact contents —
2023-09-01T00:05:14Z,ec2,StartInstances,increase,4.27
2023-09-01T00:15:51Z,ec2,StopInstances,decrease,-1.89
2023-09-01T00:45:07Z,s3,PutObject,increase,0.01
2023-09-01T01:12:33Z,rds,CreateDBInstance,increase,1.20
2023-09-01T01:30:05Z,rds,DeleteDBInstance,decrease,-1.20
2023-09-01T02:05:00Z,ec2,RunInstances,increase,6.34
2023-09-01T02:50:49Z,lambda,InvokeFunction,increase,0.0002
2023-09-01T03:15:30Z,ec2,TerminateInstances,decrease,-4.27
2023-09-01T04:22:10Z,rds,ModifyDBInstance,increase,0.58
2023-09-01T05:02:45Z,s3,DeleteObject,decrease,-0.01

Lines that must be present in the filtered file (in the same order):
1. 2023-09-01T00:05:14Z,ec2,StartInstances,increase,4.27
2. 2023-09-01T01:12:33Z,rds,CreateDBInstance,increase,1.20
3. 2023-09-01T02:05:00Z,ec2,RunInstances,increase,6.34
4. 2023-09-01T04:22:10Z,rds,ModifyDBInstance,increase,0.58

Thus, after correct execution:

/home/user/finops/filtered/usage-2023-09-increase.log   → contains the 4 lines above, newline-terminated.
/home/user/finops/summary/increase-count.txt            → contains the single line: 4⏎
name: task_000000_53732e2a
